description: Voices 'lei' when their differences form a pleasing or workable accord. Parts of a design or participants in a shared activity can do the same without becoming uniform. 'shemoli' (harmonize) brings that relation about; 'koru' (harmonious) describes it.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l', let air pass along its sides, and move into stressed middle-height front unrounded 'e'. Keep high-front unrounded 'i' in its own syllable across the 'e.i' hiatus by raising the tongue without inserting a glide.
sound_symbolism: The vowels remain separate across the hiatus, each keeping its own shape while the voice carries through both. Two distinct sounds sharing one voiced movement make a ready mnemonic for harmony.
usage_notes: 'lei' is intransitive: the voices, parts, or participants that harmonize form its subject. In noun position it is harmony. Transitive 'shemoli' names the deliberate adjustment that brings parts into accord.

semantic_domains.aesthetic: distinct sounds or forms fitting into a pleasing whole
semantic_domains.community: participants maintaining a workable accord without becoming uniform
